(A) There are Competitive and Non-competitive inhibitor drugs.

(B) These can bind at the active sites and allosteric sites.

(C) Competitive Drugs are allosteric site blocking drugs.

(D) Non-competitive Drugs are active site blocking drugs.

Choose the correct answer from the options given below
question:
Statements about Enzyme Inhibitor Drugs are given below :

(A) There are Competitive and Non-competitive inhibitor drugs.

(B) These can bind at the active sites and allosteric sites.

(C) Competitive Drugs are allosteric site blocking drugs.

(D) Non-competitive Drugs are active site blocking drugs.

Choose the correct answer from the options given below
solution: Enzyme inhibitors are molecules that interact with enzymes (temporary or permanent) in some way and reduce the rate of an enzyme-catalyzed reaction or prevent enzymes to work in a normal manner. Enzyme inhibitors can be competitive inhibitors (inhibit the attachment of substrate on active site of enzyme) and non-competitive inhibitor (changes the active site of enzyme after binding at allosteric site.) So, statement A and B are correct. But statement C and D are incorrect.

(C) Ar and Kr

(D) I and At

solution:
Rb & Cs have nearly same electron gain enthalpy Their values are nearly =-46 kJ/mol

Ar & Kr also have same ΔHe.g.. Value is +96 kJ/mol.

Na and K have different values (-53 and -48 kJ/mol) . And I and At has values of -295 and -270 kJ/mol. 

As we move down the group value of electron gain enthalpy decreases, with few exceptions.

solution:

A spiro compound, or spirane, is a chemical compound, typically an organic compound, that presents a twisted structure of two or more rings (a ring system), in which 2 or 3 rings are linked together by one common atom.

Bicyclo compounds are a class of saturated compounds consisting of two fused rings, having two or more atoms in common, and that take the name of an open chain hydrocarbon containing the same total number of atoms.

Aromatic compounds are chemical compounds that consist of conjugated planar ring systems accompanied by delocalised pi-electron clouds in place of individual alternating double and single bonds.

Heterocyclic compounds are cyclic compounds with the ring containing carbon and other element, the component being oxygen, nitrogen and sulphur.

(D) Record the time immediately before the appearance of blue colour.

solution:
For kinetic study of the reaction of iodide ion with H2O2 at room temperature following Precautions needs to be used.

  • Always keep the concentration of sodium thiosulphate solution less than that of potassium iodide solution.
  • Always use freshly prepared starch solution.
  • Use fresh samples of hydrogen peroxide and potassium iodide.
  • Always use the same measuring cylinders for measuring solutions in two different sets of observations. If after measuring one solution, the cylinder is used for measuring another solution, clean it before using.
  • Record the time immediately after the appearance of blue colour.

So, among the given statements only the statement A, B, and C are correct.

The number of inter halogens from the following having square pyramidal structure is

ClF3, IF7, BrF5, BrF3, I2Cl6, IF5, ClF, ClF5
question:
The number of inter halogens from the following having square pyramidal structure is

ClF3, IF7, BrF5, BrF3, I2Cl6, IF5, ClF, ClF5
solution:
Square pyramidal structures are BrF5,IF5 and ClF5.

These molecules are made up of 6 equally spaced sp3d2 hybrid orbitals arranged at 90o angles. The shape of the orbitals is octahedral. One orbital contains a lone pair of electrons, so the remaining five atoms connected to the central atom gives the molecule a square pyramidal shape. Among the given options the structure with five bond pairs and one lone pair is these three structures only.

options: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn></math>is the remainder when <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> is divided by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn></math>

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn></math> is the remainder when <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> is divided by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>7</mn></math>
solution:
Given, a relation R on N given by R={x,y:3x+αy is a multiple of 7},

Now for R to be reflexive ⇒xRx

⇒3x+αx=7x

⇒3+αx=7K

⇒3+α=7λ

⇒α=7λ-3=7 N+4, where K,λ,N∈I

So, when α divided by 7, remainder is 4.

Now R to be symmetric xRy⇒yRx

3x+αy=7N1,3y+αx=7N2

⇒3+αx+y=7N1+N2=7N3

Which holds when 3+α is multiple of 7

So, α=7N+4 (as did earlier)

Now, for R to be transitive

xRy & yRz⇒xRz.

⇒3x+αy=7N1 ....1 

⇒3y+αz=7N2 ......2

And  3x+αz=7N3 .....3

Now subtracting equation 3-2 we get, 

3x+7N2-3y=7N3

Now putting the value of 3x from equation 1 we get, 7N1-αy+7N2-3y=7N3

⇒7N1+N2-3+αy=7N3

⇒3+αy=7N

Which is true again when 3+α divisible by 7, i.e. when α divided by 7, remainder is 4 .

Here, centroid h,k≡cost+sint+a3,sint-cost+b3

⇒3h-a=cost+sint        ...i

Eliminating t from above two equation i & ii, we get

In two different experiments, an object of mass 5 kg moving with a speed of 25 ms-1 hits two different walls and comes to rest within
(i) 3 second, (ii) 5 seconds, respectively.
Choose the correct option out of the following :
question:
In two different experiments, an object of mass 5 kg moving with a speed of 25 ms-1 hits two different walls and comes to rest within
(i) 3 second, (ii) 5 seconds, respectively.
Choose the correct option out of the following :
options: Impulse and average force acting on the object will be same for both the cases.
Impulse will be same for both the cases but the average force will be different.
Average force will be same for both the cases but the impulse will be different.
Average force and impulse will be different for both the cases.
solution:
Impulse = Change in momentum

I=ΔP

Therefore, in both the cases the impulse is same.

Average force is defined as,

Favg =ΔPΔt

Since the time in the two cases is different, the average force will be different.

question: In the case of amplitude modulation to avoid distortion the modulation index <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi>μ</mi></mfenced></math> should be :
solution:
The modulation index of an amplitude modulated signal is defined as the measure or extent of amplitude variation about an un-modulated carrier.

Modulation index is  μ=AmAc

To avoid distortion, μ≤1 because  μ>1 will result in interference between career frequency and message frequency, resulting into distortion.

question: A wire of resistance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>R</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math>$R_{1}$ is drawn out so that its length is increased by twice of its original length. The ratio of new resistance to original resistance is:
solution:
As per the given question, the change in the length is ∆l=2l.

Resistance of a wire is given by, R=ρlA

Therefore, R1=ρl1 A1

When the length is starched by 2l1, final length will be l2=l1+2l1=3l1. As the volume remains constant l2A2=l1A1  ⇒A2=A13. Therefore,

R2=ρ3l1 A13=9ρl1 A1

∴R2R1=9

question:
The equation λ=1.227x nm can be used to find the de-Broglie wavelength of an electron. In this equation x stands for :
Where,
m= mass of electron
P= momentum of electron
K= Kinetic energy of electron
V= Accelerating potential in volts for electron
solution:
The de-Broglie's wavelength is given by λ=hmv, where, h is Planck's constant and v is velocity.

Using relation between momentum and kinetic energy, we have λ=h2 mKE

Here, kinetic energy of electron is stated as KE=qV.

Then, we have λ=h2mqV

Putting the values of m=9.1×10-31 kg and q=1.62×10-19 C of electron

We get, λ=1.22V nm.

So, x stand for V.

question: If the radius of earth shrinks by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>%</mo></math> while its mass remains same. The acceleration due to gravity on the earth's surface will approximately
solution:
The acceleration due to gravity on the surface of the earth is given by, g=GMR2.

The radius shrunk by 2%. Therefore, ∆RR×100=-2%.

As the mass is constant and the change in the radius of the earth is relatively small, we can write

∆gg=-2∆RR⇒∆gg×100=4%

Hence, the acceleration due to gravity increases by 4%.

question:
Given below are two statements:

Statement I :The average momentum of a molecule in a sample of an ideal gas depends on temperature.

Statement II : The rms speed of oxygen molecules in a gas is v. If the temperature is doubled and the oxygen molecules dissociate into oxygen atoms, the rms speed will become 2v.

options: Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true
solution:
Momentum is a vector quantity and average momentum of molecules of a sample is zero due to random motion.

Pavg =0

RMS speed of a molecule is given by

vrms=3RTM

Now the temperature is doubled Tnew =2T and due to dissociation Mnew=M2.

⇒vnew v=2TM2TM

⇒vnew=2v

question: A balloon has mass of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> in air. The air escapes from the balloon at a uniform rate with velocity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. If the balloon shrinks in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math> completely. Then, the average force acting on that balloon will be (in dyne).
solution:
Given that the mass of the air m=10 g and it escapes in time t=5 s with velocity v=4.5 cm s-1.

The average force on the balloon can be given as,

F=vdmdt

⇒F=4.5×105=9 dyne

question:

(A) decreasing the number of turns
(B) increasing the magnetic field
(C) decreasing the area of the coil
(D) decreasing the torsional constant of the spring

Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
solution:
The current in the galvanometer is calculated by i=KNABθ.

Therefore, the sensitivity of the galvanometer,

dθdi=NABK

Hence, only options B and D are correct.

question:
As shown in the figure, a metallic rod of linear density 0.45 kg m-1 is lying horizontally on a smooth incline plane which makes an angle of 45° with the horizontal. The minimum current flowing in the rod required to keep it stationary, when 0.15 T magnetic field is acting on it in the vertical upward direction, will be

(Use g=10 m s-2)
solution:
For the wire to remain stationary, the net force acting on it should be zero.

Balancing the forces along the incline,

mgsin45°=ILBcos45°

⇒I=mLgB

⇒I=0.45100.15=30 A

question: If the projection of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mover><mi>i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn><mover><mi>j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mover><mi>k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover></math> on <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mover><mi>j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mi>α</mi><mover><mi>k</mi><mo>^</mo></mover></math> is zero. Then, the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> will be
solution:
Given here:

a→=2i^+4j^-2k^ and b→=i^+2j^+αk^

Now, a→·b→=0, as a→ should be perpendicular to b→.

∴  2×1+4×2-2×α=0 

∴  α=5
